elements.sent-2411-001: We study Defant and Kravitz's generalization of Sch\"utzenberger's promotion operator to arbitrary labelings of finite posets in two directions.
elements.sent-2411-002: Defant and Kravitz showed that applying the promotion operator $n-1$ times to a labeling of a poset on $n$ elements always gives a natural labeling of the poset and called a labeling tangled if it requires the full $n-1$ promotions to reach a natural labeling.
elements.sent-2411-003: They also conjectured that there are at most $(n-1)!$ tangled labelings for any poset on $n$ elements.
elements.sent-2411-004: In the first direction, we propose a further strengthening of their conjecture by partitioning tangled labelings according to the element labeled $n-1$ and prove that this stronger conjecture holds for inflated rooted forest posets and a new class of posets called shoelace posets.
elements.sent-2411-005: In the second direction, we introduce sorting generating functions and cumulative generating functions for the number of labelings that require $k$ applications of the promotion operator to give a natural labeling.
elements.sent-2411-006: We prove that the coefficients of the cumulative generating function of the ordinal sum of antichains are log-concave and obtain a refinement of the weak order on the symmetric group.
elements.sent-2411-007: A partially ordered set $P$ on $n$ elements is \definition{naturally labeled if each element is labeled with an integer between $1$ and $n$ such that the labels respect the ordering on elements of $P$.}
elements.sent-2411-008: In 1972, Sch\"utzenberger introduced the \definition{promotion operator on natural labelings of posets \cite{schutzenberger-1972 .}}
elements.sent-2411-009: The motivation for the promotion operator comes from an earlier paper of Sch\"utzenberger \cite{Schutzenberger-1963 , in which he defines a related operator, \definition{evacuation , to study the celebrated RSK algorithm.}}
elements.sent-2411-010: Promotion and evacuation were subsequently studied by Stanley in relation to Hecke algebra products \cite{stanley-2009 , by Rhoades in relation to cyclic sieving phenomenons \cite{rhoades-2010 , and by Striker and Williams in relation to rowmotion and alternating sign matrices \cite{strikerwilliams2012 , among many others.}}}
elements.sent-2411-011: As originally defined, promotion applies only to natural labelings of posets.
elements.sent-2411-012: Defant and Kravitz considered generalizing the notion of promotion to operate on arbitrary poset labelings and referred to their generalization as \definition{extended promotion \cite{defant-kravitz-2023 .}}
elements.sent-2411-013: Given a labeling $L$ of a poset, the extended promotion of $L$ is denoted $\partial L$.
elements.sent-2411-014: A key property of extended promotion is that applying it to a labeling yields a new labeling that is ``closer'' to a natural labeling.
elements.sent-2411-015: This property is quantified precisely in the following theorem.
elements.sent-2411-016: Applied to a natural labeling of a poset, the extended promotion will always produce another natural labeling.
elements.sent-2411-017: Defant and Kravitz \cite{defant-kravitz-2023 define a tangled labeling of an $n$-element poset as a labeling that requires $n-1$ promotions to give a natural labeling.}
elements.sent-2411-018: Intuitively, the tangled labelings of a poset are those that are furthest from being ``sorted" by extended promotion; they require the full $n-1$ applications of extended promotion in \cref{theorem:defant-kravitz-main .}
elements.sent-2411-019: Defant and Kravitz studied the number of tangled labelings of a poset and conjectured the following upper bound on the number of tangled labelings.
elements.sent-2411-020: This formula was used by Hodges to show \cref{conj:main-conjecture holds for all inflated rooted forest posets.}
elements.sent-2411-021: Furthermore, Hodges conjectured a stronger version of \cref{conj:main-conjecture .}
elements.sent-2411-022: In the preprint of \cite{defant-kravitz-2023 , Defant and Kravitz proposed the following, listed as Conjecture 5.2 in \cite{defant2020promotionsorting .}}
elements.subsection-2411-002: Outline and Summary of Main Results
elements.sent-2411-025: In this paper, we study the number of tangled labelings of posets by partitioning tangled labelings according to which poset element has label $n-1$.
elements.sent-2411-026: We propose the following new conjecture.
elements.sent-2411-027: For all $x \in P$, let $|\T_x(P)|$ denote the number of tangled labelings of $P$ such that $x$ is labeled $n-1$.
elements.sent-2411-028: Then $|\T_x(P)| \le (n-2)!$ with equality if and only if there is a unique minimal element $y \in P$ such that $y <_P x$.
elements.sent-2411-029: In \cref{theorem:inflated_rooted and \cref{thm:shoelace , we prove that that the $(n-2)!$ conjecture holds for inflated rooted forest posets and for a new class of posets that we call shoelace posets.}}
elements.sent-2411-030: Furthermore, the conjecture has been computationally verified on all posets with nine or fewer elements.
elements.sent-2411-031: Following \cite{hodges-2022 , we also consider the sorting time for labelings that are not tangled and introduce associated generating functions.}
elements.sent-2411-032: In \cref{rmk:false , we give a poset on six elements that is a counterexample to \cref{false .}}
elements.sent-2411-033: Our results completely determine the generating functions for ordinal sums of antichains.
elements.sent-2411-034: We introduce a related generating function called the cumulative generating function and prove log-concavity of the cumulative generating function for ordinal sums of antichains.
elements.sent-2411-035: In \cref{section:promotion-properties we review the basic properties of extended promotion.}
elements.sent-2411-036: In \cref{section:inflated-rooted-forests we prove that inflated rooted forest posets satisfy the $(n-2)!$ conjecture.}
elements.sent-2411-037: In \cref{section:shoelace-posets we prove that inflated shoelace posets satisfy the $(n-2)!$ conjecture and give an exact enumeration for the number of tangled labelings of a particular type of shoelace poset called a $W$-poset.}
elements.sent-2411-038: In \cref{section:generating-functions we study the generating function of the sorting time of labelings of the ordinal sum of a poset $P$ with the antichain $T_k$ on $k$ elements.}
elements.sent-2411-039: In \cref{sec:ordinalsum we show that the cumulative generating function for ordinal sums of antichains are log-concave and use the cumulative generating functions to introduce a new partial order on the symmetric group $\mathfrak{S _n$.}}
elements.sent-2411-040: In \cref{section:future-work we propose future directions to explore.}

elements.sent-2411-047: A poset $P$ is said to be \definition{connected if its Hasse diagram is a connected graph.}
